name: Spanda
shortDescription: Spanda, in Kashmir Shaivism, refers to the divine creative vibration or throb of consciousness. It is the dynamic aspect of ultimate reality, the movement within stillness.
description: Spanda, a central concept in Kashmir Shaivism, signifies the divine creative vibration or throb of consciousness. It is the inherent dynamism that exists within the static, absolute reality of Shiva. Spanda is not merely movement or change, but rather the very energy that gives rise to the universe and all its manifestations. It is the pulsation of awareness, the subtle stirring within the unmanifest that allows for the emergence of the manifest world. This throb is considered to be the source of all activity, both internal and external, and is the foundation of all experience. Understanding Spanda allows one to perceive the underlying unity and interconnectedness of all things. It is the 'first movement' of consciousness, the initial impulse of creation that continues to resonate throughout existence. Practices such as mantra recitation, meditation, and contemplation are used to recognize and align oneself with the Spanda, ultimately leading to liberation and the realization of one's true nature as one with the divine. Spanda is closely linked to the concept of Nada-Brahman, the sound-essence of the universe, and is often associated with the power of Shabda (word/sound) and Bija-mantras (seed syllables), which are believed to be potent expressions of this creative energy. It is considered the life force of the universe, constantly pulsating and giving rise to new forms and experiences. The realization of Spanda is not a mere intellectual understanding but a direct experience of the vibrant, dynamic energy that permeates all of reality.

sources.title: The Doctrine of Vibration: An Analysis of the Doctrines and Practices of Kashmir Shaivism
sources.author: Mark S. G. Dyczkowski
